title: Directors in consultancy services
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate accounting, investing, banking, insurance, securities, and other financial activities of a branch, office, or department of an establishment. Direct financial activities, such as planning, procurement, and investments for all or part of an organization. Plan, direct, or coordinate investment strategy or operations for a large pool of liquid assets supplied by institutional investors or individual investors. (Based on 18 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Administer compensation, benefits, and performance management systems, and safety and recreation programs.
Prepare training budget for department or organization and develop methods to improve employment policies.
Plan, develop, and provide training and staff development programs using various effective methods.
Analyze training needs to develop or improve existing training programs and train instructors in related techniques.
Administer, direct, and review employee benefit programs, and design policies to meet legal requirements.

Evaluate needs for procurement of funds and provide guidance on financial objectives to management.
Train staff in the use of products, programs, or activities and company procedures or policies.
Schedule staff or supervise scheduling and assign tasks and work hours.
Coordinate facility schedules to maximize usage and efficiency, and plan programs of events or schedules of activities.
Establish budgets and financial goals, including writing budgets for recreational activities or programs.
Perform accounting duties, such as recording cash flow, preparing bank deposits, or generating financial statements, and calculating department expenses and revenue.
Recruit, interview, or hire employees and train workers in company procedures or policy.
Review regulatory agency submission materials to ensure accuracy, timeliness, compliance, and adherence to regulatory agency requirements.
Monitor operations to ensure compliance with applicable health, safety, or hygiene standards, and verify that all regulatory policies have been communicated and implemented.
Provide recommendations and solutions in crisis situations, such as workplace violence, protests, and demonstrations.
Maintain documentation of compliance activities, including complaints received and investigation outcomes, and prepare management reports.
Identify potential for loss and develop strategies to eliminate it.
Administer systems and programs to reduce loss, maintain inventory control, or increase safety.
Develop relationships and communicate with customers, site managers, developers, land owners, authorities, utility representatives, or residents.
Supervise employees or subcontractors to ensure quality of work or adherence to safety regulations or policies.
Estimate costs associated with operations, including repairs or preventive maintenance.
Prepare wind field operational budgets.
Review, negotiate, or approve wind farm contracts.
Monitor project milestones and deliverables.
Monitor costs incurred by project staff to identify budget issues.
Communicate with key stakeholders to determine project requirements and objectives.
